619	\subsection{The three cocycle obligations}
620	
621	\begin{lemma}[Self-component collapses to the identity (C1 + counit triangle)]
622	  \label{lem:gr_glueChartComponent_self_counit}
623	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueChartComponent_self_counit}
624	  \uses{def:gr_glueChartComponent, def:scheme_modules_glue,
625	    lem:gr_restrictIsoPullback_congr, lem:gr_restrictAdjunction_unit_iso,
626	    lem:gr_restrictIsoPullback_counit, lem:gr_overlapBaseChange_inv_app,
627	    lem:gr_pullbackCongr_hom_eqToHom}
628	  The \((i,i)\)-component \cref{def:gr_glueChartComponent}, transposed back along the
629	  chart immersion \(\iota_i\) (post-composed with the geometric counit), is the
630	  identity of \(\mathcal{M}_i\):
631	  \[
632	    \mathrm{s}_i^{(i)}\ \circ\ \varepsilon^{\mathrm{geo}}_{\mathcal{M}_i}
633	      \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{M}_i}.
634	  \]
635	  This is the second descent obligation, consuming the self-identity (C1).
636	\end{lemma}
637	\begin{proof}
638	  \uses{def:gr_glueChartComponent, def:scheme_modules_glue,
639	    lem:gr_restrictIsoPullback_congr, lem:gr_restrictAdjunction_unit_iso,
640	    lem:gr_restrictIsoPullback_counit, lem:gr_overlapBaseChange_inv_app,
641	    lem:gr_pullbackCongr_hom_eqToHom}
642	  Since \(f_{ii}\) factors as \(t_{ii}\circ f_{ii}\) (because \(t_{ii} = \mathrm{id}\)),
643	  (C1) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} says the transition \(g_{ii}\) is the
644	  canonical congruence cast; through \cref{lem:gr_pullbackCongr_hom_eqToHom} and
645	  \cref{lem:gr_restrictIsoPullback_congr} the three middle factors of the component
646	  collapse to a single site-level congruence cast. Rewriting the geometric unit by
647	  \cref{lem:gr_restrictAdjunction_unit_iso} and the geometric counit by
648	  \cref{lem:gr_restrictIsoPullback_counit} turns the whole composite into the
649	  site-level unit, the congruence cast, the section form of \(\beta_{ii}^{-1}\)
650	  (\cref{lem:gr_overlapBaseChange_inv_app}), and the site-level counit. On sections
651	  over an open this is a cycle of presheaf restrictions along opens identities whose
652	  composite open-inclusion is the identity; folding the restrictions and using
653	  proof-irrelevance of the open inclusions gives the identity.
654	\end{proof}
655	
656	\begin{lemma}[Transpose of the overlap base change (mate core)]
657	  \label{lem:gr_glueOverlapFactor_transpose}
658	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueOverlapFactor_transpose}
659	  \uses{def:gr_glueOverlapFactorIso, lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso,
660	    lem:gr_overlapBaseChange_hom_app, def:scheme_modules_glue}
661	  The adjoint transpose along \(\iota_i\) of the pullback-form overlap base change
662	  \(\gamma_{ij}\) (\cref{def:gr_glueOverlapFactorIso}) is the canonical four-functor
663	  comparison of the glue square:
664	  \[
665	    \widehat{\gamma_{ij}}
666	      \;=\;
667	    (\iota_j)_{*}\bigl(\eta_{t_{ij}\circ f_{ji}}\bigr)
668	      \ \circ\ \mathrm{pushComp}
669	      \ \circ\ \mathrm{pushCongr}
670	      \ \circ\ \mathrm{pushComp}^{-1},
671	  \]
672	  the unit along \(t_{ij}\circ f_{ji}\) pushed forward along \(\iota_j\), regrouped by
673	  the pushforward-composition comparison, reindexed by the pushforward-congruence along
674	  the glue condition \((t_{ij}\circ f_{ji})\circ \iota_j = f_{ij}\circ \iota_i\), and
675	  ungrouped by the inverse pushforward-composition. The identity is free of the
676	  cocycle hypotheses: it is pure site-level content.
677	\end{lemma}
678	\begin{proof}
679	  \uses{def:gr_glueOverlapFactorIso, lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso,
680	    lem:gr_overlapBaseChange_hom_app, def:scheme_modules_glue,
681	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
682	  Expand the adjoint transpose of \(\gamma_{ij}\): the left-adjoint comparison
683	  isomorphisms (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}) conjugating the
684	  chart factor cancel against the unit/counit, leaving the bare site-level overlap base
685	  change \(\beta_{ij}\) (\cref{lem:glueOverlapBaseChangeIso}). The unit bridge at
686	  \(t_{ij}\circ f_{ji}\) and the three naturality squares of the pushforward-composition
687	  and pushforward-congruence comparisons rewrite the transpose into the stated
688	  four-factor composite up to whiskering and reassociation. The residual site-level
689	  core is checked on sections over an open: by \cref{lem:gr_overlapBaseChange_hom_app}
690	  the section form of \(\beta_{ij}\) is a presheaf restriction along an opens identity,
691	  and the remaining composite is a cycle of such restrictions whose total open
692	  inclusion is the identity, folded by proof-irrelevance exactly as in the closing step
693	  of \cref{lem:gr_glueChartComponent_self_counit}.
694	\end{proof}
695	
696	\begin{lemma}[Mate recognition of the factor transpose]
697	  \label{lem:gr_glueOverlapFactor_mate}
698	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueOverlapFactor_mate}
699	  \uses{lem:gr_glueOverlapFactor_transpose, def:gr_glueOverlapFactorIso,
700	    def:scheme_modules_glue}
701	  For any morphism \(m : \mathcal{W} \to (\iota_j)_{*}\mathcal{M}_j\) on the glued
702	  scheme, the conjugated transport of the inverse transpose of \(m\) along the glue
703	  square equals the inverse transpose, along \(f_{ij}\), of \(\iota_i^{*}(m)\) followed
704	  by \(\gamma_{ij}\) (\cref{def:gr_glueOverlapFactorIso}). In other words, the factor
705	  transpose \cref{lem:gr_glueOverlapFactor_transpose} is the mate of \(\gamma_{ij}\)
706	  through the pseudofunctor structure of pullback.
707	\end{lemma}
708	\begin{proof}
709	  \uses{lem:gr_glueOverlapFactor_transpose, def:gr_glueOverlapFactorIso,
710	    def:scheme_modules_glue}
711	  Both sides are computed by transposing twice. By injectivity of the two transpose
712	  bijections it suffices to compare the double transposes. Naturality of the transpose
713	  in its left argument peels \(m\) off the right-hand side; the conjugate of the
714	  pullback-composition isomorphism along the glue condition is the inverse
715	  pushforward-composition isomorphism; and folding the unit pair and reindexing along
716	  the glue condition of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} reduces the identity to the
717	  factor transpose \cref{lem:gr_glueOverlapFactor_transpose}.
718	\end{proof}
719	
720	\begin{lemma}[Overlap compatibility of the restriction morphisms]
721	  \label{lem:gr_glueRestriction_overlap_compat}
722	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueRestriction_overlap_compat}
723	  \uses{lem:glueRestrictionHom, def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_glue_equalizer}
724	  The \((i,j)\)-component of the descent-equalizer condition, transposed to the
725	  pullback level, states that over the overlap \(V_{ij}\) the two restriction
726	  morphisms \(\mathrm{r}_i\) and \(\mathrm{r}_j\) (\cref{lem:glueRestrictionHom}) differ
727	  exactly by the transition isomorphism \(g_{ij}\) (through the pseudofunctor
728	  congruence casts).
729	\end{lemma}
730	\begin{proof}
731	  \uses{lem:glueRestrictionHom, def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_glue_equalizer}
732	  The glued sheaf is the descent equalizer, so its \(i\)- and \(j\)-projections satisfy
733	  the overlap-agreement condition of \cref{def:gr_glue_equalizer}. Transposing that
734	  condition along \(\iota_i\) and \(\iota_j\) and folding the transposes back through
735	  the adjunction bijection turns it into the stated identity between the overlap
736	  pullbacks of \(\mathrm{r}_i\) and \(\mathrm{r}_j\), conjugated by the glue-condition
737	  casts of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} and corrected by \(g_{ij}\).
738	\end{proof}
739	
740	\begin{lemma}[Pair condition transposed: restriction composed with the component]
741	  \label{lem:gr_glueRestrictionHom_glueChartComponent}
742	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionHom_glueChartComponent}
743	  \uses{lem:gr_glueRestriction_overlap_compat, lem:gr_glueOverlapFactor_mate,
744	    def:gr_glueChartComponent, lem:glueRestrictionHom}
745	  The chart restriction morphism \(\mathrm{r}_i\) (\cref{lem:glueRestrictionHom})
746	  followed by the \(j\)-th candidate-inverse component \cref{def:gr_glueChartComponent}
747	  equals the restricted \(j\)-th projection:
748	  \[
749	    \mathrm{r}_i\ \circ\ \mathrm{s}_i^{(j)}
750	      \;=\; \iota_i^{*}(\pi_j).
751	  \]
752	  This is the third descent obligation; together with
753	  \cref{lem:gr_glueRestrictionInv_proj} and \cref{lem:gr_glueRestrict_hom_ext} it gives
754	  \(\mathrm{r}_i \circ \mathrm{s}_i = \mathrm{id}\).
755	\end{lemma}
756	\begin{proof}
757	  \uses{lem:gr_glueRestriction_overlap_compat, lem:gr_glueOverlapFactor_mate,
758	    def:gr_glueChartComponent, lem:glueRestrictionHom}
759	  Unfold the component \cref{def:gr_glueChartComponent}. By the overlap compatibility
760	  \cref{lem:gr_glueRestriction_overlap_compat} the overlap pullback of \(\mathrm{r}_i\)
761	  transported across \(g_{ij}\) is the inverse mate transpose of the restricted
762	  projection \(\iota_i^{*}(\pi_j)\) through \(\gamma_{ij}\)
763	  (\cref{lem:gr_glueOverlapFactor_mate}). Substituting this into the unit-naturality
764	  square of \(\mathrm{r}_i\) along \(f_{ij}\) and folding the resulting transpose
765	  cancels the factor isomorphism, leaving exactly \(\iota_i^{*}(\pi_j)\).
766	\end{proof}

768	\subsection{Joint faithfulness of chart restrictions}
769	
770	\begin{lemma}[Chart restrictions are jointly faithful]
771	  \label{lem:gr_pullback_jointly_faithful}
772	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback_map_jointly_faithful}
773	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib,
774	    def:scheme_modules_glue}
775	  Two morphisms \(u, v : \mathcal{W} \to \mathcal{W}'\) of sheaves of modules on the
776	  glued scheme that agree after pullback to every chart, \(\iota_i^{*}u = \iota_i^{*}v\)
777	  for all \(i\), are equal. This realises the restriction-uniqueness core of the
778	  characterisation of the glued sheaf up to unique isomorphism: a morphism out of the
779	  glued sheaf is determined chart-locally.
780	\end{lemma}
781	\begin{proof}
782	  \uses{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
783	  Transfer the chart agreement to the site-level restriction functor by conjugating
784	  with the comparison \cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}, whose
785	  sections are concrete: \(\Gamma(\iota_i^{\mathrm{res}}\mathcal{W}, V) =
786	  \Gamma(\mathcal{W}, \iota_i(V))\). Then test \(u\) and \(v\) on sections over any
787	  open \(O\) of the glued scheme using separatedness of the target sheaf
788	  (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}) over the cover \(\{\iota_i(\iota_i^{-1}O)\}\)
789	  of \(O\) — which is a cover by joint surjectivity of the chart immersions
790	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}). On each cover piece the agreement is the chart
791	  identity moved inside the sections by naturality of \(u, v\) on the presheaf level.
792	\end{proof}
793	
794	\begin{definition}
795	
796	\leanok
797	[The restriction isomorphism of the glued sheaf]
798	  \label{def:glueRestrictionIso}
799	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionIso}
800	  % NOTE: this is the 1:1 home for the Lean decl `glueRestrictionIso`
801	  % (AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GlueDescent.lean). The block
802	  % `def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso` in Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex is a stale
803	  % forward-declaration pinning the SAME Lean target and should be reconciled by the
804	  % plan agent (flagged in the writer report) so the pin is not duplicated.
805	  \uses{thm:isIso_glueRestrictionHom}
806	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be the glued sheaf of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}, with the
807	  transition family \((g_{ij})\) satisfying (C1)/(C2). For each chart index \(i\) the
808	  restriction morphism \(\mathrm{r}_i\) (\cref{lem:glueRestrictionHom}) is promoted to
809	  the \emph{restriction isomorphism}
810	  \[
811	    \rho_i \;:\; \iota_i^{*}\,\mathcal{M} \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{M}_i,
812	  \]
813	  the canonical identification of the chart restriction of the glued sheaf with the
814	  \(i\)-th input sheaf, packaged from \cref{thm:isIso_glueRestrictionHom}. Its
815	  underlying morphism is the adjoint transpose of the \(i\)-th descent-equalizer
816	  projection, and over each overlap \(V_{ij}\) the two restrictions \(\rho_i, \rho_j\)
817	  differ exactly by the transition isomorphism \(g_{ij}\).
818	\end{definition}

18	/-! ## Gluing a sheaf of modules along a scheme glue datum
19	
20	`Scheme.Modules.glue` descends a sheaf of modules from per-chart data plus a transition
21	cocycle over a `Scheme.GlueData`. Mathlib carries no turn-key module descent over a
22	scheme glue datum (confirmed), so this is an Archon-original construction.
23	
24	Construction (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue`): the glued sheaf is built directly as a
25	categorical limit — an **equalizer of pushforwards** — rather than a hand-built presheaf of
26	compatible families. Concretely, `glue` forms the two parallel maps
27	`∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{i,j} (ιᵢ ≫ f_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)` (one leg the adjunction unit composed
28	with the pushforward-composition comparison, the other transported across the inverse
29	transition `(g_ij)⁻¹`), and takes their equalizer inside `Scheme.Modules D.glued`. The
30	self-identity (C1) and triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) hypotheses `_hC1`/`_hC2` on the
31	family `g` are NOT consumed in forming the equalizer object (the limit exists for any family
32	of transition maps); they are the descent conditions pinned down downstream when the
33	restriction isomorphisms are produced. The body below and the `_hC1`/`_hC2` signature are
34	complete (axiom-clean since iter-056). -/

38	/-! ### Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap
39	
40	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
41	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
42	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
43	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
44	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
45	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
46	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/

48	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**

50	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
51	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating

219	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
220	
221	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
222	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
223	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
224	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
225	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
226	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
227	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
228	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
229	morphisms. -/
230	
231	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
232	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
233	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
234	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
235	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
236	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
237	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
238	  constructor
239	  intro V
240	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
241	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
242	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
243	  apply zigzag_isConnected
244	  intro s t
245	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
246	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
247	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
248	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
249	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))
250	
251	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
252	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
253	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
254	Project-local. -/
255	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
256	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
257	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
258	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
259	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I
260

262	`pullbackFreeIso`s related by the `eqToHom` transport of their (differing) sources.
263	Project-local — used for the bundle-transition self-identity. -/
264	lemma pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) (I : Type u) :
265	    eqToHom (congrArg
266	        (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) h)
267	        ≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
268	      = (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom := by
269	  subst h; simp

272	`pullbackFreeIso φ ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ).symm` is the `eqToIso` transport between the

276	lemma pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
277	    (I : Type u) :
278	    pullbackFreeIso φ I ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).symm
279	      = eqToIso (congrArg
280	          (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I))

284	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on

287	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free

289	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.

291	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
292	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
293	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
294	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h

362	/-- **Mate compatibility of `homEquiv`.** For adjunctions `adj₁ : L₁ ⊣ R₁`, `adj₂ : L₂ ⊣ R₂`
363	and a natural transformation `α : L₂ ⟶ L₁`, transposing `α.app c ≫ f` under `adj₂` equals
364	transposing `f` under `adj₁` post-composed with the conjugate transformation
365	(`CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α`) evaluated at `d`. Project-local; derived from
366	`CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv` + naturality of the conjugate transformation. -/
367	lemma homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app {𝒞 𝒟 : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category 𝒞]
368	    [CategoryTheory.Category 𝒟] {L₁ L₂ : 𝒞 ⥤ 𝒟} {R₁ R₂ : 𝒟 ⥤ 𝒞}
369	    (adj₁ : L₁ ⊣ R₁) (adj₂ : L₂ ⊣ R₂) (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) {c : 𝒞} {d : 𝒟}
370	    (f : L₁.obj c ⟶ d) :
371	    adj₂.homEquiv c d (α.app c ≫ f)
372	      = adj₁.homEquiv c d f ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app d := by
373	  -- `rw` is unreliable at locating these right-associated sub-composites, so we assemble the
374	  -- proof entirely from term-mode whiskering equalities and chain them with `.trans`.
375	  have h1 := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α c
376	  -- the two `homEquiv_unit` expansions, with all implicits fixed by the stated types.
377	  have huA : adj₂.homEquiv c d (α.app c ≫ f)
378	      = adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (α.app c ≫ f) :=
379	    Adjunction.homEquiv_unit adj₂ c d (α.app c ≫ f)
380	  have huB : adj₁.homEquiv c d f = adj₁.unit.app c ≫ R₁.map f :=
381	    Adjunction.homEquiv_unit adj₁ c d f
382	  -- LHS transpose, in left-bracketed shape.
383	  have e1 : adj₂.homEquiv c d (α.app c ≫ f)
384	      = (adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (α.app c)) ≫ R₂.map f :=
385	    huA.trans <| (CategoryTheory.whisker_eq (adj₂.unit.app c) (R₂.map_comp (α.app c) f)).trans
386	      (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
387	  -- RHS transpose, in the same left-bracketed shape.
388	  have e2 : adj₁.homEquiv c d f ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app d
389	      = (adj₁.unit.app c ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app (L₁.obj c))
390	          ≫ R₂.map f :=
391	    (CategoryTheory.eq_whisker huB
392	        ((CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app d)).trans <|
393	      (Category.assoc _ _ _).trans <|
394	        (CategoryTheory.whisker_eq (adj₁.unit.app c)
395	          ((CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).naturality f)).trans
396	          (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
397	  exact e1.trans ((CategoryTheory.eq_whisker h1.symm (R₂.map f)).trans e2.symm)

1334	/-! ### The candidate inverse of the chart restriction morphism
1335	
1336	The inverse `σ_i : M i ⟶ ι_i^* (glue …)` is assembled as an equalizer lift: the chart
1337	pullback preserves the descent equalizer (`glueRestrictEqualizerIso`) and the
1338	pushforward product (`glueRestrictProdIso`), so a map into `ι_i^* (glue …)` is a
1339	family of maps into the factors `ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j)` equalizing the restricted legs.
1340	The `j`-th component (`glueChartComponent`) transports a section of `M i` to the
1341	overlap: the unit along `f_ij`, the transition isomorphism `g_ij`, then the inverse of
1342	the overlap base change `β_ij` (`glueOverlapBaseChangeIso`, in pullback form
1343	`glueOverlapFactorIso`). The three named obligations consuming the cocycle hypotheses
1344	are `glueChartFamily_equalizes` (C2 transported), `glueChartComponent_self_counit`
1345	(C1 + the counit triangle), and `glueRestrictionHom_glueChartComponent` (the
1346	pair-`(i,j)` equalizer condition transposed). -/
1347	
1348	section GlueRestrictionInverse
1349	
1350	variable (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
1351	
1352	/-- **Object-level overlap base change in pullback form**: the `β_ij` of
1353	`glueOverlapBaseChangeIso`, evaluated at `M j` and conjugated through
1354	`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` on both sides, identifying
1355	`ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j) ≅ (f_ij)_* ((t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* M_j)` with the geometric pullback
1356	functors. Project-local. -/
1357	noncomputable def glueOverlapFactorIso (i j : D.J) :
1358	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).obj (M j))
1359	      ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f i j)).obj
1360	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) :=
1361	  (restrictFunctorIsoPullback (D.ι i)).symm.app
1362	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).obj (M j)) ≪≫

1945	PROOF ROUTE (scoped iter-066, partially built): the chart pullback `ι_i^*` preserves
1946	limits (`pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion` — it is isomorphic to the
1947	site-level pushforward `restrictFunctor`), so `ι_i^* (glue …)` is the equalizer of the
1948	restricted legs `ι_i^* (glueLegA)`, `ι_i^* (glueLegB)` and the restricted product
1949	embeds into `∏_j ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j)`. The candidate inverse `M i ⟶ ι_i^* (glue …)`
1950	is the equalizer lift of the family whose `j`-component transports a section of `M i`
1951	to the overlap: `unit_{f_ij} ≫ (f_ij)_* (g_ij-conjugate) ≫ β_ij⁻¹`, where
1952	`β_ij : ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j) ≅ (f_ij)_* ((t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* M_j)` is the open-cover base

766	`f_ij^* (c i) = congr ∘ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* (c j) ∘ g_ij⁻¹` does (all comparisons through the
767	pseudofunctor casts). Both legs are transposed along the composite overlap immersion via
768	`homEquiv_comp_unit_pushforwardComp` / `homEquiv_comp_pushforwardCongr`, and the
769	hom-equivalence is injective. Project-local. -/
770	lemma glueLift_cond_iff (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u})
771	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
772	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
773	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
774	    {W : D.glued.Modules}
775	    (c : ∀ i, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj W ⟶ M i) (i j : D.J) :
776	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι i)).homEquiv W (M i) (c i) ≫
777	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).map
778	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f i j)).unit.app (M i)) ≫
779	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f i j) (D.ι i)).hom.app
780	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i)))
781	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι j)).homEquiv W (M j) (c j) ≫
782	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).map
783	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
784	            (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).unit.app (M j)) ≫
785	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).hom.app
786	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) ≫
787	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
788	          ((D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j)).map (g i j).inv ≫
789	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
790	          (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
791	            rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).hom.app
792	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i))))
793	    ↔ ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (D.f i j) (D.ι i)).inv.app W ≫
794	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).map (c i)
795	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
796	            (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
797	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).inv.app W ≫
798	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).inv.app W ≫
799	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).map (c j) ≫ (g i j).inv) := by
800	  -- transpose the left leg
801	  rw [homEquiv_comp_unit_pushforwardComp (D.f i j) (D.ι i) (c i)]
802	  -- transpose the right leg: regroup, fire the leg transpose, absorb `(g i j).inv`
803	  -- into the transpose, then fire the congruence-cast transpose
804	  have hR : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι j)).homEquiv W (M j) (c j) ≫
805	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).map
806	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
807	            (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).unit.app (M j)) ≫
808	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).hom.app
809	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) ≫
810	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
811	          ((D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j)).map (g i j).inv ≫
812	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
813	          (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
814	            rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).hom.app
815	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i)))
816	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f i j ≫ D.ι i)).homEquiv W
817	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i))
818	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
819	              (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
820	                rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).inv.app W ≫
821	            (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).inv.app W ≫
822	              (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).map (c j)) ≫ (g i j).inv)) := by
823	    calc (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι j)).homEquiv W (M j) (c j) ≫
824	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).map
825	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
826	              (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).unit.app (M j)) ≫
827	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).hom.app
828	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) ≫
829	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
830	            ((D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j)).map (g i j).inv ≫
831	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
832	            (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
833	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).hom.app
834	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i)))
835	        = (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι j)).homEquiv W (M j) (c j) ≫
836	            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι j)).map
837	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
838	                (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).unit.app (M j)) ≫
839	            (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).hom.app
840	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)))) ≫
841	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
842	            ((D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j)).map (g i j).inv) ≫
843	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
844	            (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
845	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).hom.app
846	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i)) := by
847	          simp only [Category.assoc]
848	      _ = (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
849	              ((D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j)).homEquiv W
850	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
851	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).inv.app W ≫
852	                (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).map (c j))) ≫
853	            (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
854	              ((D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j)).map (g i j).inv) ≫
855	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
856	            (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
857	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).hom.app
858	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i)) :=
859	          congrArg (fun m => (m ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
860	              ((D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j)).map (g i j).inv) ≫
861	            (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
862	              (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
863	                rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).hom.app
864	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i)))
865	            (homEquiv_comp_unit_pushforwardComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j) (c j))
866	      _ = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
867	              ((D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j)).homEquiv W
868	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i))
869	              ((((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).inv.app W ≫
870	                (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).map (c j))) ≫ (g i j).inv)) ≫
871	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
872	            (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
873	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).hom.app
874	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i)) :=
875	          congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
876	              (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
877	                rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).hom.app
878	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i)))
879	            (Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right _ _ _).symm
880	      _ = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f i j ≫ D.ι i)).homEquiv W
881	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i))
882	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
883	                (show (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ≫ D.ι j = D.f i j ≫ D.ι i by
884	                  rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition i j)).inv.app W ≫
885	              (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (D.ι j)).inv.app W ≫
886	                (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).map (c j)) ≫ (g i j).inv)) :=
887	          homEquiv_comp_pushforwardCongr _ _
888	  rw [hR, Equiv.apply_eq_iff_eq]
889	  simp only [Category.assoc]

891	/-! ### Restriction of the glued sheaf to a chart (`def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`)
892	
893	The glued sheaf is the descent equalizer `eq(a, b) ⊆ ∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ`. This section
894	re-exposes the two legs of that equalizer as standalone declarations
895	(`glueLegA`/`glueLegB`), records that `glue` *is* their equalizer (`glueIsoEqualizer`,
896	definitional), and produces the canonical projection `glueProj i` of the glued sheaf
897	onto the `i`-th pushforward factor together with its compatibility with `glueLift`.
898	The adjoint transpose of `glueProj i` along the chart immersion `D.ι i` is the
899	*restriction morphism* `glueRestrictionHom i : ιᵢ^* (glue …) ⟶ M i`; effective descent

900	(consuming the cocycle hypotheses C1/C2) makes it an isomorphism — the restriction
901	isomorphism `glueRestrictionIso` of `def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`. The
902	limit-preservation engine is `restrictFunctor`: pullback along an open immersion is
903	naturally isomorphic to a site-level pushforward, which is a right adjoint, hence
904	preserves the descent equalizer and the pushforward product. -/
905	
906	/-- `restrictFunctor f` along an open immersion is a right adjoint: it is a site-level
907	pushforward of sheaves of modules, whose left adjoint (the site-level pullback) exists
908	by the presheaf-pullback + sheafification construction. Project-local instance. -/
909	instance restrictFunctor_isRightAdjoint {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
910	    (restrictFunctor f).IsRightAdjoint := by
911	  delta restrictFunctor
912	  -- bare `infer_instance` fails on the outer search; the explicit presheaf-pullback +
913	  -- sheafification construction elaborates (its three instance hypotheses all resolve)
914	  exact (SheafOfModules.PullbackConstruction.adjunction _).isRightAdjoint
915	
916	/-- `restrictFunctor f` along an open immersion preserves limits (it is a right
917	adjoint). Project-local. -/
918	noncomputable instance restrictFunctor_preservesLimits.{w, w'} {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
919	    (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
920	    PreservesLimitsOfSize.{w, w'} (restrictFunctor f) :=
921	  (Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (restrictFunctor f)).rightAdjoint_preservesLimits
922	
923	/-- **Pullback of sheaves of modules along an open immersion preserves limits**: it is
924	naturally isomorphic to `restrictFunctor f`, a site-level pushforward and right
925	adjoint. This is the engine that lets the chart restriction commute with the descent
926	equalizer. Project-local. -/
927	instance pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion.{w, w'} {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
928	    (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
929	    PreservesLimitsOfSize.{w, w'} (Scheme.Modules.pullback f) :=
930	  preservesLimits_of_natIso (restrictFunctorIsoPullback f)
931	
932	section GlueRestriction
933	
934	-- NOTE: `glue`/`glueLift` elaborated universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}`
935	-- (their universe was pinned during elaboration); the restriction layer follows suit.
936	variable (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
937	
938	/-- The product of pushforwards `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ` into which the glued sheaf embeds.
939	Project-local helper re-exposing the `P` of `glue`. -/
940	noncomputable def glueProd : D.glued.Modules :=
941	  ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)
942	
943	/-- The overlap product `∏_{(i,j)} (f_ij ≫ ιᵢ)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)` receiving the two descent
944	legs. Project-local helper re-exposing the `Qfun`-product of `glue`. -/
945	noncomputable def glueOverlapProd : D.glued.Modules :=
946	  ∏ᶜ fun p : D.J × D.J =>
947	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
948	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
949	
950	/-- First descent leg (`a` of `glue`): on the `(i,j)`-component, restrict the `i`-th
951	chart section to the overlap `V (i,j)` via the unit of the pullback–pushforward
952	adjunction along `f_ij` and the pushforward-composition comparison. Project-local. -/
953	noncomputable def glueLegA : glueProd D M ⟶ glueOverlapProd D M :=
954	  Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫
955	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
956	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
957	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
958	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
959	
960	/-- Second descent leg (`b` of `glue`): on the `(i,j)`-component, restrict the `j`-th
961	chart section, transport it across the transition isomorphism `g_ij`, and reindex the
962	immersion via the glue condition. Project-local. -/
963	noncomputable def glueLegB
964	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
965	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j)) :
966	    glueProd D M ⟶ glueOverlapProd D M :=
967	  Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫
968	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
969	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
970	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
971	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
972	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
973	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
974	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
975	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
976	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
977	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
978	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
979	
980	variable (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
981	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
982	  (hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
983	      (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
984	  (hC2 : ∀ i j k,
985	      pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
986	          (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
987	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
988	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
989	          (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
990	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
991	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
992	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
993	          (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k))
994	
995	/-- The glued sheaf *is* the equalizer of the two (re-exposed) descent legs. The
996	isomorphism is definitional (`Iso.refl`); it exists to let the equalizer API fire
997	syntactically on `glue`. Project-local. -/
998	noncomputable def glueIsoEqualizer :
999	    glue D M g hC1 hC2 ≅ equalizer (glueLegA D M) (glueLegB D M g) :=
1000	  Iso.refl _
1001	
1002	/-- Projection of the glued sheaf onto the `i`-th pushforward factor `(ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ`:
1003	the equalizer inclusion followed by the product projection. Project-local. -/
1004	noncomputable def glueProj (i : D.J) :
1005	    glue D M g hC1 hC2 ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i) :=
1006	  (glueIsoEqualizer D M g hC1 hC2).hom ≫ equalizer.ι (glueLegA D M) (glueLegB D M g) ≫
1007	    Pi.π _ i
1008	
1009	/-- `glueLift` followed by the `i`-th projection recovers the `i`-th component of the
1010	lifted family. Project-local. -/
1011	@[reassoc]
1012	lemma glueLift_glueProj {W : D.glued.Modules}
1013	    (k : ∀ i, W ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i))
1014	    (hk : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
1015	      k p.1 ≫
1016	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
1017	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
1018	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
1019	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
1020	        = k p.2 ≫
1021	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
1022	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
1023	              (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
1024	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
1025	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
1026	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
1027	            ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
1028	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
1029	            (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
1030	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
1031	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))) (i : D.J) :
1032	    glueLift D M g hC1 hC2 k hk ≫ glueProj D M g hC1 hC2 i = k i := by
1033	  dsimp only [glueLift, glueProj, glueIsoEqualizer, Iso.refl_hom]
1034	  -- term-mode: the mixed-provenance comp nodes block positional `rw [Category.id_comp]`
1035	  exact (congrArg (equalizer.lift (Pi.lift k) _ ≫ ·) (Category.id_comp _)).trans
1036	    ((Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
1037	      ((congrArg (· ≫ Pi.π _ i) (equalizer.lift_ι _ _)).trans (Limits.Pi.lift_π _ _)))
1038	
1039	/-- **The restriction morphism of the glued sheaf** to the `i`-th chart: the adjoint
1040	transpose, along the chart immersion `ιᵢ`, of the `i`-th projection `glueProj i`.
1041	Effective descent (`isIso_glueRestrictionHom`) makes it an isomorphism. Project-local. -/
1042	noncomputable def glueRestrictionHom (i : D.J) :
1043	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glue D M g hC1 hC2) ⟶ M i :=
1044	  ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.ι i)).homEquiv _ _).symm
1045	    (glueProj D M g hC1 hC2 i)
1046	
1047	/-- **The chart restriction of the glued sheaf is the equalizer of the restricted
1048	descent legs**: the chart pullback preserves the descent equalizer
1049	(`pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion`). First reduction step of
1050	`isIso_glueRestrictionHom`. Project-local. -/
1051	noncomputable def glueRestrictEqualizerIso (i : D.J) :
1052	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).obj (glue D M g hC1 hC2)
1053	      ≅ equalizer ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueLegA D M))
1054	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueLegB D M g)) :=
1055	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).mapIso (glueIsoEqualizer D M g hC1 hC2) ≪≫
1056	    PreservesEqualizer.iso (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)) _ _
1057	
1058	/-- **The chart restriction of the pushforward product is the product of the
1059	restrictions**: the chart pullback preserves the product
1060	(`pullback_preservesLimits_of_isOpenImmersion`). Second reduction step of
1061	`isIso_glueRestrictionHom`: the factors `ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j)` are then identified with
1062	`(f_ij)_* ((t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* M_j)` by the overlap base change of the cartesian chart

lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
  simp [← Hom.comp_app]

end Presheaf

noncomputable section Functorial

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _

section

attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts

instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

end

variable (X) in
/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

variable (X) in
/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _

variable (X) in
lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _

/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
def pushforwardComp :
    pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
def pullbackComp :
    pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _

/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)

lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
========
def pseudofunctor :
    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))

end Functorial

noncomputable section Restriction

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]

/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
but has better defeqs. -/
def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
  (restrictFunctor f).obj M

@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
  · ext U x
    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
    exact congr($this x)

instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)

instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl

/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)

/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)

@[simp]

lemma glueChartFamily_pullback_map_π (i j : D.J) :

      = glueChartComponent D M g i j := by
  have hπ : (glueRestrictProdIso D M i).hom ≫ Pi.π _ j
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map
          (Pi.π (fun k => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι k)).obj (M k)) j) :=
    piComparison_comp_π _ _ _
  rw [glueChartFamily, Category.assoc, ← hπ, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  exact Limits.Pi.lift_π _ _

/-- **Pair component of the equalizing condition** (the `(p,q)`-component of
`glueChartFamily_equalizes`, C2 transported): the `p`-th candidate-inverse component
followed by the restricted first descent-leg factor equals the `q`-th component
followed by the restricted second descent-leg factor. This is where the triple-overlap
multiplicativity (C2) at the triple `(i,p,q)` is consumed, transposed along the
triple-overlap immersion `q_pq : V_ipq ⟶ U_i`; the degenerate pairs (`p = i` or
`q = i`) instead invoke the self-identity (C1). Project-local. -/
lemma glueChartComponent_leg_compat (i p q : D.J) :
    glueChartComponent D M g i p ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p)).map

      = glueChartComponent D M g i q ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι q)).map

                (D.t p q ≫ D.f q p)).unit.app (M q)) ≫

                rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p q)).hom.app
              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p q)).obj (M p))) := by

  --  (b) a triple `β`: `(f_pq ≫ ι_p)_* ⋙ restrictFunctor (ι_i) ≅

  --      recipe over (a), with the section-level compatibility proved by the

  --  (c) the transposed component equation then IS `hC2 i p q` conjugated by the
  --      `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`s — the bridges `glueData_bridge_src/mid/tgt`
  --      were designed so the endpoints line up.
  -- The degenerate pairs (`p = i` or `q = i`) additionally use `hC1` through
  -- `pullbackCongr_hom_app_eqToHom` (as in `glueChartComponent_self_counit`).

include hC1 hC2 in
/-- **The candidate-inverse family equalizes the restricted descent legs**
(C2 transported): the `(p,q)`-component of the equalizing condition is the
triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) at the triple `(i,p,q)`, transported through the
overlap base changes `β`. Obligation 1 of `isIso_glueRestrictionHom`. Project-local. -/
lemma glueChartFamily_equalizes (i : D.J) :
    glueChartFamily D M g i ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueLegA D M)
      = glueChartFamily D M g i ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueLegB D M g) := by
  -- componentwise detection through the preserved overlap product (the same
  -- `piComparison` discipline as `glueRestrict_proj_compat`/`glueRestrict_hom_ext`)
  rw [← Iso.cancel_iso_hom_right _ _
    (PreservesProduct.iso (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)) fun p : D.J × D.J =>
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))]
  apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
  intro p
  simp only [Category.assoc]
  have hπ : (PreservesProduct.iso (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)) fun p : D.J × D.J =>
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))).hom ≫ Pi.π _ p
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (Pi.π _ p) := piComparison_comp_π _ _ _
  rw [hπ]
  -- fold each leg against the projection (`Pi.lift_π` at the glued level), splitting
  -- off the `p.1`-/`p.2`-th chart projection (term-mode `map_comp` under the diamond)
  have hA : (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueLegA D M) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (Pi.π _ p)
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map
          (Pi.π (fun k => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι k)).obj (M k)) p.1) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app
                (M p.1)) ≫
            (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))) :=
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
      ((congrArg (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (Limits.Pi.lift_π _ _)).trans
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map_comp _ _))
  have hB : (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (glueLegB D M g) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (Pi.π _ p)
      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map
          (Pi.π (fun k => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι k)).obj (M k)) p.2) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map
          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
              ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
                (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
            (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
            (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
              ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
            (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
              (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
                rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))) :=
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
      ((congrArg (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map (Limits.Pi.lift_π _ _)).trans
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.ι i)).map_comp _ _))
  rw [reassoc_of% hA, reassoc_of% hB,
    glueChartFamily_pullback_map_π_assoc D M g i p.1,
    glueChartFamily_pullback_map_π_assoc D M g i p.2]
  exact glueChartComponent_leg_compat D M g hC1 hC2 i p.1 p.2

input.description: Read isOpenEmbedding context and pullback instance section variables

content:
refine SheafedSpace.forgetToPresheafedSpace.preimageIso ?_
    apply PresheafedSpace.IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq (PresheafedSpace.ofRestrict _ _) f.1
    exact Subtype.range_coe_subtype

end LocallyRingedSpace.IsOpenImmersion

theorem IsOpenImmersion.isOpen_range {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [H : IsOpenImmersion f] :
    IsOpen (Set.range f) :=
  H.base_open.isOpen_range

namespace Scheme.Hom

variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [H : IsOpenImmersion f]

theorem isOpenEmbedding : IsOpenEmbedding f :=
  H.base_open

/-- The image of an open immersion as an open set. -/
@[simps]
def opensRange : Y.Opens :=
  ⟨_, f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpen_range⟩

@[simp]
theorem mem_opensRange {f : X ⟶ Y} [IsOpenImmersion f] {y : Y} :
    y ∈ opensRange f ↔ ∃ x, f x = y := .rfl

/-- The functor `opens X ⥤ opens Y` associated with an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`. -/
def opensFunctor : X.Opens ⥤ Y.Opens :=
  LocallyRingedSpace.IsOpenImmersion.opensFunctor f.toLRSHom

/-- `f ''ᵁ U` is notation for the image (as an open set) of `U` under an open immersion `f`.
instance : PreservesLimit (cospan f g) forget :=
  CategoryTheory.preservesLimit_of_createsLimit_and_hasLimit _ _

instance : PreservesLimit (cospan g f) forget :=
  preservesPullback_symmetry _ _ _

instance hasPullback_of_left : HasPullback f g :=
  hasLimit_of_created (cospan f g) forget

instance hasPullback_of_right : HasPullback g f :=
  hasLimit_of_created (cospan g f) forget

instance : IsOpenImmersion (pullback.snd f g) := by
  have := PreservesPullback.iso_hom_snd forget f g
  dsimp only [Scheme.forgetToLocallyRingedSpace, inducedFunctor_map] at this
  change LocallyRingedSpace.IsOpenImmersion _
  rw [← this]
  infer_instance

instance : IsOpenImmersion (pullback.fst g f) := by
  rw [← pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_snd]
  infer_instance

instance [IsOpenImmersion g] :
    IsOpenImmersion (limit.π (cospan f g) WalkingCospan.one) := by
  rw [← limit.w (cospan f g) WalkingCospan.Hom.inl]
  change IsOpenImmersion (_ ≫ f)
  infer_instance

instance : PreservesLimit (cospan f g) Scheme.forgetToTop := by
  delta Scheme.forgetToTop
  refine @Limits.comp_preservesLimit _ _ _ _ _ _ (K := cospan f g) _ _ (F := forget)
    (G := LocallyRingedSpace.forgetToTop) ?_ ?_
  · infer_instance
  refine @preservesLimit_of_iso_diagram _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (diagramIsoCospan.{u} _).symm ?_
  dsimp [LocallyRingedSpace.forgetToTop]

/-- Hom-side companion of `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso_inv_app_app`. Project-local. -/
lemma glueOverlapBaseChangeIso_hom_app_app (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i j : D.J)
    (N : (D.U j).Modules) (V : (D.U i).Opens) :
    (((glueOverlapBaseChangeIso D i j).hom.app N).app V :
        Γ(N, (D.ι j) ⁻¹ᵁ ((D.ι i) ''ᵁ V)) ⟶ Γ(N, (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ''ᵁ ((D.f i j) ⁻¹ᵁ V)))
      = N.presheaf.map (eqToHom (glueData_preimage_image_eq D i j V).symm).op := by
  ext x
  rfl

/-! ### Bridges between the geometric and the site-level adjunction
input.new_string:
/-- Hom-side companion of `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso_inv_app_app`. Project-local. -/
lemma glueOverlapBaseChangeIso_hom_app_app (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i j : D.J)
    (N : (D.U j).Modules) (V : (D.U i).Opens) :
    (((glueOverlapBaseChangeIso D i j).hom.app N).app V :
        Γ(N, (D.ι j) ⁻¹ᵁ ((D.ι i) ''ᵁ V)) ⟶ Γ(N, (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) ''ᵁ ((D.f i j) ⁻¹ᵁ V)))
      = N.presheaf.map (eqToHom (glueData_preimage_image_eq D i j V).symm).op := by
  ext x
  rfl

/-! ### Triple-overlap base change

The `(p,q)`-component of the equalizing condition for the candidate inverse on the
chart `U_i` lives on the triple overlap `V_ipq := V_ip ×_{U_i} V_iq`. This block
mirrors the pair-level overlap base change (`glueData_preimage_image_eq` →
`glueOverlapBaseChangeIso`) for the square

  `V_ipq ──τ──→ V_pq`         `τ   := t'_ipq ≫ fst : V_ipq ⟶ V_pq`
  `  │q             │f_pq ≫ ι_p`
  `U_i ──ι_i──→ glued`,        `q   := fst ≫ f_ip  : V_ipq ⟶ U_i`

which commutes (`glueData_triple_square`) and is cartesian on underlying opens
(`glueData_preimage_image_eq₃`). -/

/-- Commutativity of the triple-overlap square: the triple overlap maps into the glued
scheme the same way through the chart `U_i` (via `q = fst ≫ f_ip`) and through the
pair overlap `V_pq` (via the cocycle transport `τ = t'_ipq ≫ fst` and `f_pq ≫ ι_p`).
Consequence of `glueData_bridge_mid` and the glue condition. Project-local. -/
theorem glueData_triple_square (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i p q : D.J) :
    (D.t' i p q ≫ pullback.fst (D.f p q) (D.f p i)) ≫ (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p)
      = (pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ≫ D.f i p) ≫ D.ι i := by
  rw [← Category.assoc, ← glueData_bridge_mid D i p q]
  simp only [Category.assoc]
  rw [D.glue_condition i p]

/-- **Triple-overlap opens identity**: for an open `V` of the chart `U_i`, the preimage
under `f_pq ≫ ι_p : V_pq ⟶ X` of its image in the glued scheme is the image, under the
cocycle transport `τ = t'_ipq ≫ fst`, of its preimage under `q = fst ≫ f_ip`. The
underlying-opens cartesianness of the triple-overlap square; triple analogue of
`glueData_preimage_image_eq` (via `ι_eq_iff` at the pairs `(i,p)` and `(i,q)`, the
point-range of `pullback.fst`, and injectivity of the open immersion `f_pq`).
Project-local. -/
lemma glueData_preimage_image_eq₃ (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i p q : D.J)
    (V : (D.U i).Opens) :
    (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p) ⁻¹ᵁ ((D.ι i) ''ᵁ V)
      = (D.t' i p q ≫ pullback.fst (D.f p q) (D.f p i)) ''ᵁ
          ((pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ≫ D.f i p) ⁻¹ᵁ V) := by
  ext x
  constructor
  · intro hx
    obtain ⟨y, hyV, hyx⟩ := hx
    -- `y` is glued to `f_pq x` through the overlap `V_ip` …
    obtain ⟨w₁, hw₁, hw₁'⟩ := (D.ι_eq_iff i p y ((D.f p q) x)).mp hyx
    -- … and to `(t_pq ≫ f_qp) x` through `V_iq` (via the glue condition at `(p,q)`)
    have hgl : (D.ι i) y = (D.ι q) ((D.t p q ≫ D.f q p) x) :=
      hyx.trans (congrArg (fun m : D.V (p, q) ⟶ D.glued => m x)
        ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans (D.glue_condition p q))).symm
    obtain ⟨w₂, hw₂, _⟩ := (D.ι_eq_iff i q y ((D.t p q ≫ D.f q p) x)).mp hgl
    -- hence `w₁` lifts to the triple overlap `V_ipq`
    have hw₁_range : w₁ ∈ Set.range (pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q)) := by
      rw [IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackFst (D.f i q) (D.f i p)]
      exact ⟨w₂, hw₂.trans hw₁.symm⟩
    obtain ⟨w, hw⟩ := hw₁_range
    refine ⟨w, ?_, ?_⟩
    · -- the lift lies over `V`
      show (D.f i p) ((pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q)) w) ∈ V
      rw [hw, hw₁]; exact hyV
    · -- and transports to `x`: compare under the injective open immersion `f_pq`
      apply (D.f p q).isOpenEmbedding.injective
      exact (congrArg (fun m : pullback (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ⟶ D.U p => m w)
          (glueData_bridge_mid D i p q)).symm.trans
        ((congrArg (D.t i p ≫ D.f p i) hw).trans hw₁')
  · rintro ⟨w, hwV, rfl⟩
    refine ⟨(pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ≫ D.f i p) w, hwV, ?_⟩
    exact congrArg (fun m : pullback (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ⟶ D.glued => m w)
      (glueData_triple_square D i p q).symm

/-- **The two composite opens functors of the triple-overlap square are equal**: going
"into the glued scheme along `ι_i` and back down to `V_pq`" is the same site functor
as "down to the triple overlap along `q = fst ≫ f_ip` and across along
`τ = t'_ipq ≫ fst`". Object-level content is `glueData_preimage_image_eq₃`; morphisms
are proof-irrelevant in the opens preorder. Triple analogue of
`glueData_overlap_opensFunctor_eq`. Project-local. -/
lemma glueData_triple_opensFunctor_eq (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i p q : D.J) :
    (D.ι i).opensFunctor ⋙ TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p).base
      = TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ≫ D.f i p).base ⋙
          (D.t' i p q ≫ pullback.fst (D.f p q) (D.f p i)).opensFunctor :=
  CategoryTheory.Functor.ext (fun V => glueData_preimage_image_eq₃ D i p q V)
    (fun _ _ _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _)

/-- **Structure-sheaf compatibility of the triple-overlap square**: the two composite
section maps `Γ(U_i, V) ⟶ Γ(V_pq, τ ''ᵁ (q ⁻¹ᵁ V))` — "through the glued scheme" and
"through the triple overlap" — coincide. Both are the `appLE` of the two (equal)
composites of the square (`glueData_triple_square`); triple analogue of
`glueData_overlap_appIso_compat`. Project-local. -/
lemma glueData_triple_appIso_compat (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i p q : D.J)
    (V : (D.U i).Opens) :
    ((D.ι i).appIso V).inv ≫ (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p).app ((D.ι i) ''ᵁ V) ≫
        (D.V (p, q)).presheaf.map
          (eqToHom (glueData_preimage_image_eq₃ D i p q V).symm).op
      = (pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ≫ D.f i p).app V ≫
        ((D.t' i p q ≫ pullback.fst (D.f p q) (D.f p i)).appIso
          ((pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ≫ D.f i p) ⁻¹ᵁ V)).inv := by
  rw [Iso.eq_comp_inv]
  simp only [Category.assoc]
  rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq]
  simp only [Scheme.Hom.appIso_hom', Scheme.Hom.app_eq_appLE,
    Scheme.Hom.appLE_map_assoc, Scheme.Hom.appLE_comp_appLE]
  exact appLE_congr_mor (glueData_triple_square D i p q) _ _ _ _

/-- **Triple-overlap base change** (`β_ipq`): restricting a pushforward
`(f_pq ≫ ι_p)_* N` (of a sheaf on the pair overlap `V_pq`) to the chart `U_i` is the
same as restricting `N` to the triple overlap `V_ipq` along `τ = t'_ipq ≫ fst` and
pushing forward along `q = fst ≫ f_ip`. Same four-factor recipe as
`glueOverlapBaseChangeIso` over the opens identity `glueData_preimage_image_eq₃`.
Project-local. -/
noncomputable def glueTripleBaseChangeIso (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i p q : D.J) :
    Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p) ⋙ restrictFunctor (D.ι i)
      ≅ restrictFunctor (D.t' i p q ≫ pullback.fst (D.f p q) (D.f p i)) ⋙
        Scheme.Modules.pushforward (pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ≫ D.f i p) :=
  haveI h₁ : Functor.IsContinuous
      ((D.ι i).opensFunctor ⋙ TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p).base)
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(D.U i))
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(D.V (p, q))) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(D.glued)) _
  haveI h₂ : Functor.IsContinuous
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ≫ D.f i p).base ⋙
        (D.t' i p q ≫ pullback.fst (D.f p q) (D.f p i)).opensFunctor)
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(D.U i))
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(D.V (p, q))) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(pullback (D.f i p) (D.f i q))) _
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _
      (NatIso.ofComponents
        (fun V => eqToIso (glueData_preimage_image_eq₃ D i p q V).symm)
        (fun _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _)) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by
      ext V x
      exact congr($(glueData_triple_appIso_compat D i p q (unop V)) x)) ≪≫
    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

/-- Sections of the triple-overlap base change, inverse side: on sections over
`V ⊆ U_i`, the inverse of `β_ipq` is the restriction map of `N` along the opens
identity `glueData_preimage_image_eq₃`. Project-local. -/
lemma glueTripleBaseChangeIso_inv_app_app (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i p q : D.J)
    (N : (D.V (p, q)).Modules) (V : (D.U i).Opens) :
    (((glueTripleBaseChangeIso D i p q).inv.app N).app V :
        Γ(N, (D.t' i p q ≫ pullback.fst (D.f p q) (D.f p i)) ''ᵁ
            ((pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ≫ D.f i p) ⁻¹ᵁ V))
          ⟶ Γ(N, (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p) ⁻¹ᵁ ((D.ι i) ''ᵁ V)))
      = N.presheaf.map (eqToHom (glueData_preimage_image_eq₃ D i p q V)).op := by
  ext x
  rfl

/-- Hom-side companion of `glueTripleBaseChangeIso_inv_app_app`. Project-local. -/
lemma glueTripleBaseChangeIso_hom_app_app (D : Scheme.GlueData.{0}) (i p q : D.J)
    (N : (D.V (p, q)).Modules) (V : (D.U i).Opens) :
    (((glueTripleBaseChangeIso D i p q).hom.app N).app V :
        Γ(N, (D.f p q ≫ D.ι p) ⁻¹ᵁ ((D.ι i) ''ᵁ V))
          ⟶ Γ(N, (D.t' i p q ≫ pullback.fst (D.f p q) (D.f p i)) ''ᵁ
            ((pullback.fst (D.f i p) (D.f i q) ≫ D.f i p) ⁻¹ᵁ V)))
      = N.presheaf.map (eqToHom (glueData_preimage_image_eq₃ D i p q V).symm).op := by
  ext x
  rfl

/-! ### Bridges between the geometric and the site-level adjunction

1493	The inverse `σ_i : M i ⟶ ι_i^* (glue …)` is assembled as an equalizer lift: the chart
1494	pullback preserves the descent equalizer (`glueRestrictEqualizerIso`) and the
1495	pushforward product (`glueRestrictProdIso`), so a map into `ι_i^* (glue …)` is a
1496	family of maps into the factors `ι_i^* ((ι_j)_* M_j)` equalizing the restricted legs.
1497	The `j`-th component (`glueChartComponent`) transports a section of `M i` to the
1498	overlap: the unit along `f_ij`, the transition isomorphism `g_ij`, then the inverse of

1500	`glueOverlapFactorIso`). The three named obligations consuming the cocycle hypotheses
1501	are `glueChartFamily_equalizes` (C2 transported), `glueChartComponent_self_counit`
1502	(C1 + the counit triangle), and `glueRestrictionHom_glueChartComponent` (the
